subject: using WARP to extract subvolumes from AREtomo tomograms
body:
hi all,

I know this must sound ridiculous, but I'm wondering whether it's possible 
to somehow provide WARP with an AREtomo reconstruction then do subtomo 
extraction on it.  I guess not, since WARP does "on-the-fly" subtomo 
generation using either frames or averaged/aligned movies.   This is 
fundamentally different than packages which simply crop the tomogram to 
generate subvolumes (e.g. dynamo?). Since my understanding is that AREtomo 
will produce better subtomos than WARP early on, I could use those 
subvolumes to obtain orientations then later switch back to the 
WARP/RELION/M pipeline. 

Second question: is the on-the-fly processing done from the frames or from 
the aligned/averaged movies.  I ask because if it's the latter can I 
conceivably alter contrast of some of those averages and have that take 
affect in the tomo/subtomo recon?

thanks!  

Jesse
